rationale: The cited paper is used in the methodology to justify and design a key construct, interstore complementarity. The authors explicitly draw on Rooderkerk et al.'s dissimilarity and dominance dimensions, explain them, and map them to their own store-level context. They also state a specific adaptation of the metric relative to Rooderkerk et al., showing direct methodological use rather than a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Rooderkerk et al. (2011)'s dissimilarity/dominance logic from assortment context effects to construct its own store-complementarity measure in a multiple-store shopping model, with a modified distance formulation.
